## 2. SQLite Database Schema

### 2.1 Core Reference Tables

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- ROLES
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE roles (
    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    code        TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,       -- 'PRE_SALES_REP', 'VAN_SALES_REP', etc.
    name        TEXT NOT NULL,              -- 'Pre-Sales Representative'
    level       TEXT NOT NULL,              -- 'FIELD', 'SUPERVISOR', 'MANAGER', 'DIRECTOR'
    created_at  TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

-- ============================================================
-- EMPLOYEES
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE employees (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    employee_code   TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    name            TEXT NOT NULL,
    role_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES roles(id),
    territory_id    INTEGER REFERENCES territories(id),
    supervisor_id   INTEGER REFERENCES employees(id),
    base_salary     REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    is_active       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

-- ============================================================
-- TERRITORIES
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE territories (
    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    code        TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    name        TEXT NOT NULL,
    region      TEXT NOT NULL,              -- 'NORTH', 'SOUTH', 'EAST', 'WEST'
    parent_id   INTEGER REFERENCES territories(id),
    created_at  TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

-- ============================================================
-- PRODUCTS (simplified product hierarchy)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE products (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    sku             TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    name            TEXT NOT NULL,
    category        TEXT NOT NULL,           -- 'Beverages', 'Snacks', 'Dairy', etc.
    sub_category    TEXT,
    brand           TEXT NOT NULL,
    is_strategic    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    is_new_launch   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    unit_price      REAL NOT NULL,
    tags            TEXT,                    -- JSON array: ["premium","focus_sku"]
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

-- ============================================================
-- CUSTOMERS (simplified customer hierarchy)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE customers (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    customer_code   TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    name            TEXT NOT NULL,
    type            TEXT NOT NULL,           -- 'GENERAL_TRADE', 'MODERN_TRADE', 'KEY_ACCOUNT', 'WHOLESALE'
    channel         TEXT NOT NULL,           -- 'RETAIL', 'HORECA', 'INSTITUTION'
    territory_id    INTEGER REFERENCES territories(id),
    credit_limit    REAL DEFAULT 0,
    tags            TEXT,                    -- JSON array: ["premium_outlet","chain_store"]
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
```

### 2.2 KPI Library

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- KPI DEFINITIONS (the master library of ~15 prototype KPIs)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE kpi_definitions (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    code            TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,        -- 'TOTAL_SALES_VALUE', 'ECO', etc.
    name            TEXT NOT NULL,               -- 'Total Sales Value'
    category        TEXT NOT NULL,               -- 'SALES', 'DISTRIBUTION', 'PRODUCTIVITY',
                                                 -- 'FINANCIAL', 'MERCHANDISING', 'ASSET',
                                                 -- 'TRADE_MARKETING', 'INVENTORY', 'BEHAVIORAL'
    description     TEXT,
    unit            TEXT NOT NULL,               -- 'CURRENCY', 'PERCENTAGE', 'COUNT', 'RATIO'
    direction       TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'HIGHER_IS_BETTER',  -- or 'LOWER_IS_BETTER'
    formula         TEXT,                        -- Human-readable formula description
    data_source     TEXT NOT NULL,               -- 'TRANSACTION', 'VISIT', 'COLLECTION', 'MANUAL'
    applicable_roles TEXT NOT NULL,              -- JSON array: ["PRE_SALES_REP","VAN_SALES_REP"]
    is_active       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
```

### 2.3 Commission Plans

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- COMMISSION PLANS (the header)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE commission_plans (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    name            TEXT NOT NULL,
    version         INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    status          TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DRAFT',  -- 'DRAFT','ACTIVE','EXPIRED','ARCHIVED'
    valid_from      TEXT NOT NULL,                   -- date
    valid_to        TEXT NOT NULL,                   -- date
    frequency       TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'MONTHLY', -- 'MONTHLY','QUARTERLY'
    description     TEXT,
    created_by      TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    parent_plan_id  INTEGER REFERENCES commission_plans(id)  -- for versioning lineage
);

-- ============================================================
-- PLAN ROLE MAPPING (which roles this plan applies to)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE plan_roles (
    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    role_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES roles(id),
    UNIQUE(plan_id, role_id)
);

-- ============================================================
-- PLAN TERRITORY MAPPING
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE plan_territories (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    territory_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES territories(id),
    UNIQUE(plan_id, territory_id)
);

-- ============================================================
-- PLAN KPI CONFIG (KPIs attached to a plan, with targets & weights)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE plan_kpis (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    kpi_id          INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES kpi_definitions(id),
    target_value    REAL NOT NULL,               -- the 100% target
    weight          REAL NOT NULL,               -- e.g., 0.25 for 25%
    min_threshold   REAL DEFAULT 0,              -- below this = 0 achievement
    max_cap_pct     REAL DEFAULT 200,            -- achievement cap (200 = 200%)
    base_payout     REAL NOT NULL,               -- payout at 100% achievement
    sort_order      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    UNIQUE(plan_id, kpi_id)
);
```

### 2.4 Slab Configuration

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- SLAB SETS (a named set of slabs for a plan_kpi)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE slab_sets (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_kpi_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES plan_kpis(id),
    slab_type       TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'STEP', 'PROGRESSIVE', 'ACCELERATOR',
                                               -- 'DECELERATOR', 'REVERSE_PENALTY'
    name            TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

-- ============================================================
-- SLAB TIERS (the individual tiers within a slab set)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE slab_tiers (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    slab_set_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES slab_sets(id),
    tier_order      INTEGER NOT NULL,          -- 1, 2, 3... (sequential, no gaps)
    range_from      REAL NOT NULL,             -- achievement % start (inclusive)
    range_to        REAL,                      -- achievement % end (exclusive); NULL = open-ended
    payout_type     TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'PERCENTAGE', 'FIXED_AMOUNT', 'RATE_PER_UNIT'
    payout_value    REAL NOT NULL,             -- the % rate, fixed amount, or per-unit rate
    label           TEXT,                      -- human label: "Bronze", "Silver", "Gold"
    UNIQUE(slab_set_id, tier_order)
);
```

### 2.5 Include/Exclude Rules

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- INCLUDE/EXCLUDE RULE SETS
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE rule_sets (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    plan_kpi_id     INTEGER REFERENCES plan_kpis(id),  -- NULL = plan-level rule
    name            TEXT NOT NULL,
    logic_mode      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'AND',        -- 'AND', 'OR'
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

-- ============================================================
-- INDIVIDUAL RULES WITHIN A RULE SET
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE rules (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    rule_set_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES rule_sets(id),
    dimension       TEXT NOT NULL,     -- 'PRODUCT', 'CUSTOMER', 'TERRITORY', 'CHANNEL',
                                       -- 'CATEGORY', 'BRAND', 'CUSTOMER_TYPE', 'TAG'
    operator        TEXT NOT NULL,     -- 'INCLUDE', 'EXCLUDE'
    field           TEXT NOT NULL,     -- 'category', 'sku', 'customer_code', 'type', 'channel', 'tag'
    match_type      TEXT NOT NULL,     -- 'EQUALS', 'IN', 'NOT_IN', 'CONTAINS', 'STARTS_WITH'
    match_values    TEXT NOT NULL,     -- JSON array: ["Beverages","Snacks"] or ["SKU001","SKU002"]
    priority        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- higher = evaluated later; EXCLUDE always wins
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
```

### 2.6 Eligibility Rules

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- ELIGIBILITY RULES (gate checks before payout)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE eligibility_rules (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    name            TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'Minimum Sales Threshold'
    metric          TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'MIN_SALES_VALUE', 'MIN_COLLECTION_PCT',
                                               -- 'MAX_RETURN_PCT', 'MIN_ACTIVE_DAYS',
                                               -- 'MIN_VISIT_COUNT'
    operator        TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'GTE', 'LTE', 'GT', 'LT', 'EQ'
    threshold_value REAL NOT NULL,             -- e.g., 50000 for min sales, 80 for min collection%
    failure_action  TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'ZERO_PAYOUT', 'PERCENTAGE_REDUCTION', 'KPI_DISQUALIFY'
    failure_value   REAL DEFAULT 0,            -- reduction %: e.g., 50 means reduce by 50%
    applies_to_kpi_id INTEGER REFERENCES plan_kpis(id),  -- NULL = entire plan
    sort_order      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
```

### 2.7 Multiplier Rules

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- MULTIPLIER RULES
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE multiplier_rules (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    name            TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'Growth Multiplier', 'Strategic SKU Bonus'
    type            TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'GROWTH', 'STRATEGIC_SKU', 'NEW_LAUNCH',
                                               -- 'TERRITORY', 'CAMPAIGN', 'ACCELERATOR'
    condition_field TEXT NOT NULL,             -- what triggers it: 'growth_pct', 'strategic_sku_pct', etc.
    condition_operator TEXT NOT NULL,          -- 'GTE', 'GT', 'BETWEEN'
    condition_value TEXT NOT NULL,             -- single value or JSON [min, max]
    multiplier_value REAL NOT NULL,            -- e.g., 1.25 = 25% boost
    stacking_mode   TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'MULTIPLICATIVE',  -- 'ADDITIVE', 'MULTIPLICATIVE', 'HIGHEST_ONLY'
    applies_to_kpi_id INTEGER REFERENCES plan_kpis(id),  -- NULL = entire plan
    sort_order      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
```

### 2.8 Penalty Rules

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- PENALTY RULES
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE penalty_rules (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    name            TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'High Return Penalty'
    trigger_metric  TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'RETURN_PCT', 'DSO', 'AUDIT_SCORE'
    trigger_operator TEXT NOT NULL,            -- 'GT', 'GTE', 'LT', 'LTE'
    trigger_value   REAL NOT NULL,             -- e.g., 10 for return_pct > 10%
    penalty_type    TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'PERCENTAGE_DEDUCTION', 'FIXED_DEDUCTION',
                                               -- 'KPI_DISQUALIFY', 'PLAN_DISQUALIFY'
    penalty_value   REAL NOT NULL,             -- deduction % or fixed amount
    applies_to_kpi_id INTEGER REFERENCES plan_kpis(id),  -- NULL = entire plan
    sort_order      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
```

### 2.9 Capping Rules

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- CAPPING RULES
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE capping_rules (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    name            TEXT NOT NULL,
    cap_type        TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'MAX_PER_KPI', 'MAX_PER_PLAN',
                                               -- 'PCT_OF_SALARY', 'PCT_OF_SALES',
                                               -- 'BUDGET_CAP', 'REGIONAL_CAP'
    cap_value       REAL NOT NULL,             -- absolute amount or percentage
    reference_field TEXT,                      -- 'base_salary', 'total_sales' (for PCT types)
    applies_to_kpi_id INTEGER REFERENCES plan_kpis(id),  -- NULL = plan-level cap
    territory_id    INTEGER REFERENCES territories(id),   -- for regional caps
    sort_order      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
```

### 2.10 Split Commission

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- SPLIT COMMISSION RULES
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE split_rules (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    name            TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'Pre-Sales & Delivery Split'
    split_type      TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'ROLE_SPLIT', 'SHARED_CREDIT', 'SUPERVISOR_OVERRIDE',
                                               -- 'POOL_DISTRIBUTION'
    source_role_id  INTEGER REFERENCES roles(id),
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

-- ============================================================
-- SPLIT PARTICIPANTS
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE split_participants (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    split_rule_id   INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES split_rules(id),
    role_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES roles(id),
    allocation_type TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'PERCENTAGE', 'FIXED'
    allocation_value REAL NOT NULL,            -- e.g., 60 for 60%
    UNIQUE(split_rule_id, role_id)
);
```

### 2.11 Transactions (Source Data)

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- SALES TRANSACTIONS (the raw data pipeline feeds)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE transactions (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    transaction_code TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    transaction_date TEXT NOT NULL,            -- date
    transaction_type TEXT NOT NULL,            -- 'SALE', 'RETURN', 'COLLECTION', 'VISIT',
                                               -- 'DELIVERY', 'CREDIT_NOTE'
    employee_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES employees(id),
    customer_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES customers(id),
    product_id      INTEGER REFERENCES products(id),       -- NULL for visits/collections
    territory_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES territories(id),
    quantity        REAL DEFAULT 0,
    unit_price      REAL DEFAULT 0,
    total_amount    REAL DEFAULT 0,
    payment_status  TEXT,                     -- 'PAID', 'PENDING', 'OVERDUE'
    days_outstanding INTEGER DEFAULT 0,       -- for DSO calculation
    is_new_customer INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    is_new_product  INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    metadata        TEXT,                     -- JSON for extensible fields
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

CREATE INDEX idx_transactions_employee ON transactions(employee_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_transactions_date ON transactions(transaction_date);
CREATE INDEX idx_transactions_type ON transactions(transaction_type);
```

### 2.12 Calculation Results

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- CALCULATION RUNS (a batch calculation execution)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE calculation_runs (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    period_start    TEXT NOT NULL,
    period_end      TEXT NOT NULL,
    status          TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',  -- 'PENDING','RUNNING','COMPLETED','FAILED'
    is_simulation   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,       -- 1 = what-if, don't persist as real
    triggered_by    TEXT NOT NULL,
    started_at      TEXT,
    completed_at    TEXT,
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

-- ============================================================
-- EMPLOYEE PAYOUTS (final per-employee per-plan result)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE employee_payouts (
    id                  INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    calculation_run_id  INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES calculation_runs(id),
    employee_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES employees(id),
    plan_id             INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    period_start        TEXT NOT NULL,
    period_end          TEXT NOT NULL,
    -- Calculation results
    gross_payout        REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   -- after all KPIs aggregated
    multiplier_amount   REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   -- total multiplier impact
    penalty_amount      REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   -- total penalty deduction
    cap_adjustment      REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   -- amount removed by capping
    net_payout          REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   -- final payable amount
    -- Eligibility
    is_eligible         INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    eligibility_failures TEXT,                      -- JSON array of failed rule names
    -- Approval
    approval_status     TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',  -- 'PENDING','MANAGER_APPROVED',
                                                          -- 'FINANCE_APPROVED','HR_APPROVED',
                                                          -- 'LOCKED','REJECTED'
    is_locked           INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    locked_at           TEXT,
    created_at          TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

-- ============================================================
-- KPI RESULTS (per-employee per-KPI breakdown within a run)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE kpi_results (
    id                  INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    employee_payout_id  INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES employee_payouts(id),
    plan_kpi_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES plan_kpis(id),
    -- Step 1-2: Scoped data
    scoped_transaction_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    -- Step 3: KPI achievement
    actual_value        REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    target_value        REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    achievement_pct     REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,      -- (actual/target)*100
    -- Step 4: Slab
    slab_tier_id        INTEGER REFERENCES slab_tiers(id),
    slab_label          TEXT,
    slab_rate           REAL DEFAULT 0,
    -- Step 5: KPI payout
    raw_kpi_payout      REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    -- Step 6: Weighted
    weight              REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    weighted_payout     REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    -- Audit details
    calculation_details TEXT,                          -- JSON: full step-by-step breakdown
    created_at          TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
```

### 2.13 Approval Workflow & Audit Trail

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- APPROVAL LOG (immutable history)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE approval_log (
    id                  INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    employee_payout_id  INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES employee_payouts(id),
    action              TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'SUBMITTED', 'MANAGER_APPROVED', 'MANAGER_REJECTED',
                                                   -- 'FINANCE_APPROVED', 'FINANCE_REJECTED',
                                                   -- 'HR_APPROVED', 'HR_REJECTED', 'LOCKED'
    performed_by        TEXT NOT NULL,             -- user/role who performed the action
    comment             TEXT,                      -- mandatory for rejections
    previous_status     TEXT NOT NULL,
    new_status          TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at          TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

-- ============================================================
-- AUDIT TRAIL (system-wide change log)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE audit_trail (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    entity_type     TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'PLAN', 'KPI', 'SLAB', 'RULE', 'PAYOUT', etc.
    entity_id       INTEGER NOT NULL,
    action          TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'CREATE', 'UPDATE', 'ACTIVATE', 'CALCULATE', etc.
    changed_by      TEXT NOT NULL,
    old_values      TEXT,                      -- JSON snapshot of previous state
    new_values      TEXT,                      -- JSON snapshot of new state
    description     TEXT,                      -- Human-readable change description
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

CREATE INDEX idx_audit_entity ON audit_trail(entity_type, entity_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_time ON audit_trail(created_at);
```

### 2.14 Simulation Snapshots

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- SIMULATION SNAPSHOTS (what-if scenarios -- never posted to real payouts)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE simulation_snapshots (
    id                  INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    calculation_run_id  INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES calculation_runs(id),
    scenario_name       TEXT NOT NULL,
    parameters          TEXT NOT NULL,          -- JSON: what-if overrides
    summary             TEXT NOT NULL,          -- JSON: budget impact, avg payout, etc.
    created_by          TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at          TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
```

## 3. Backend API Endpoints

### 3.1 Plans

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET    | `/api/plans` | List all plans (with status filter) |
| GET    | `/api/plans/:id` | Get plan with full config (KPIs, slabs, rules, etc.) |
| POST   | `/api/plans` | Create new plan |
| PUT    | `/api/plans/:id` | Update plan |
| POST   | `/api/plans/:id/clone` | Clone plan (create new version) |
| PATCH  | `/api/plans/:id/status` | Change plan status (DRAFT -> ACTIVE, etc.) |

### 3.2 KPI Library

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET    | `/api/kpis` | List all KPI definitions (filter by category, role) |
| GET    | `/api/kpis/:id` | Get single KPI definition |
| POST   | `/api/kpis` | Create new KPI definition |

### 3.3 Plan KPI Configuration

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET    | `/api/plans/:planId/kpis` | List KPIs configured for this plan |
| POST   | `/api/plans/:planId/kpis` | Add KPI to plan with target/weight/base_payout |
| PUT    | `/api/plans/:planId/kpis/:planKpiId` | Update KPI config |
| DELETE | `/api/plans/:planId/kpis/:planKpiId` | Remove KPI from plan |

### 3.4 Slab Configuration

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET    | `/api/plan-kpis/:planKpiId/slabs` | Get slab set + tiers for a plan KPI |
| POST   | `/api/plan-kpis/:planKpiId/slabs` | Create slab set with tiers |

## 4. Calculation Engine Pseudocode

This is the heart of the system. The orchestrator in `calculationPipeline.js` calls each step in non-negotiable order.

```javascript
// ======================================================================
// calculationPipeline.js -- THE ORCHESTRATOR
// ======================================================================
async function runCalculation(db, { planId, periodStart, periodEnd, isSimulation = false }) {

    // Create a calculation run record
    const run = createCalculationRun(db, { planId, periodStart, periodEnd, isSimulation });

    // Load full plan configuration
    const plan = loadPlanWithFullConfig(db, planId);
    //   plan.kpis[]         -- with slab sets, targets, weights
    //   plan.rules[]        -- include/exclude rule sets
    //   plan.eligibility[]  -- eligibility rules
    //   plan.multipliers[]  -- multiplier rules
    //   plan.penalties[]    -- penalty rules
    //   plan.caps[]         -- capping rules
    //   plan.splits[]       -- split rules

    // Get all employees this plan applies to (via role + territory mapping)
    const employees = getEligibleEmployees(db, plan);

    const allPayouts = [];

    for (const employee of employees) {
        const auditTrail = {};   // accumulates step-by-step details for this employee

        // =============================================
        // STEP 1: Fetch Scoped Transactions
        // =============================================
        let transactions = fetchTransactions(db, {
            employeeId: employee.id,
            periodStart,
            periodEnd,
        });
        auditTrail.step1 = { totalTransactions: transactions.length };

        // =============================================
        // STEP 2: Apply Mapping Filters (Include/Exclude Rules)
        // =============================================
        transactions = applyIncludeExcludeRules(transactions, plan.rules, db);
        // Rule engine logic:
        //   For each transaction:
        //     1. Check all INCLUDE rules -- must match at least one include per dimension
        //     2. Check all EXCLUDE rules -- if any exclude matches, REMOVE transaction
        //     3. EXCLUDE always overrides INCLUDE
        auditTrail.step2 = {
            filteredTransactions: transactions.length,
            excludedCount: auditTrail.step1.totalTransactions - transactions.length,
        };

        // =============================================
        // STEP 3-6: Per-KPI Calculations
        // =============================================
        const kpiResults = [];
        for (const planKpi of plan.kpis) {

            // STEP 3: Calculate KPI Achievement
            const actual = calculateKpiActual(transactions, planKpi, employee, db);
            //   Switch on KPI code:
            //     'TOTAL_SALES_VALUE'  -> SUM(amount) where type='SALE'
            //     'TOTAL_SALES_VOLUME' -> SUM(quantity) where type='SALE'
            //     'ECO'                -> COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) with sale
            //     'COLLECTION_PCT'     -> SUM(collections) / SUM(sales) * 100
            //     'RETURN_PCT'         -> SUM(returns) / SUM(sales) * 100
            //     'NEW_CUSTOMER_COUNT' -> COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) where is_new=1
            //     'LINES_PER_CALL'     -> SUM(distinct_products) / COUNT(DISTINCT visits)
            //     'DSO'                -> AVG(days_outstanding)
            //     'STRIKE_RATE'        -> orders_with_sale / total_visits * 100
            //     'STRATEGIC_SKU_PCT'  -> strategic_sku_sales / total_sales * 100
            //     etc.

            const achievementPct = (actual / planKpi.target_value) * 100;
            const clampedAchievement = Math.min(achievementPct, planKpi.max_cap_pct);

            // STEP 4: Determine Slab
            const slabResult = determineSlabTier(planKpi.slabSet, clampedAchievement);
            //   slabEngine logic:
            //     STEP slab:        find tier where achievement is in [range_from, range_to)
            //                       payout = tier.payout_value (flat rate for the entire amount)
            //     PROGRESSIVE slab: for each tier, calculate payout on the portion within that tier
            //                       total = sum of (portion_in_tier * tier.rate)
            //     ACCELERATOR:      if achievement > 100%, apply accelerated rate on excess
            //     DECELERATOR:      if achievement < threshold, apply decelerated (lower) rate
            //     REVERSE_PENALTY:  negative payout for below-minimum tiers

            // STEP 5: Calculate KPI Payout
            let kpiPayout;
            if (slabResult.payoutType === 'PERCENTAGE') {
                kpiPayout = planKpi.base_payout * (slabResult.effectiveRate / 100);
            } else if (slabResult.payoutType === 'FIXED_AMOUNT') {
                kpiPayout = slabResult.effectiveRate;
            } else if (slabResult.payoutType === 'RATE_PER_UNIT') {
                kpiPayout = actual * slabResult.effectiveRate;
            }

            // STEP 6: Apply Weight
            const weightedPayout = kpiPayout * planKpi.weight;

            kpiResults.push({
                planKpiId: planKpi.id,
                actual,
                target: planKpi.target_value,
                achievementPct: clampedAchievement,
                slabTierId: slabResult.tierId,
                slabLabel: slabResult.label,
                slabRate: slabResult.effectiveRate,
                rawKpiPayout: kpiPayout,
                weight: planKpi.weight,
                weightedPayout,
            });
        }
        auditTrail.step3to6 = kpiResults;

        // =============================================
        // STEP 7: Aggregate KPIs
        // =============================================
        let grossPayout = kpiResults.reduce((sum, r) => sum + r.weightedPayout, 0);
        auditTrail.step7 = { grossPayout };

        // =============================================
        // STEP 8: Apply Multipliers
        // =============================================
        const multiplierResult = applyMultipliers(grossPayout, plan.multipliers, employee, transactions, db);
        //   multiplierEngine logic:
        //     1. Evaluate each multiplier's condition against actual data
        //     2. Collect all triggered multipliers
        //     3. Apply stacking mode:
        //        ADDITIVE:       effectiveMultiplier = 1 + sum(each bonus - 1)
        //                        e.g., 1.15 + 1.10 => 1 + 0.15 + 0.10 = 1.25
        //        MULTIPLICATIVE: effectiveMultiplier = product(all multipliers)
        //                        e.g., 1.15 * 1.10 = 1.265
        //        HIGHEST_ONLY:   effectiveMultiplier = max(all multipliers)
        //     4. payoutAfterMultiplier = grossPayout * effectiveMultiplier

        let payoutAfterMultiplier = multiplierResult.adjustedPayout;
        let multiplierAmount = payoutAfterMultiplier - grossPayout;
        auditTrail.step8 = multiplierResult;

        // =============================================
        // STEP 9: Apply Penalties
        // =============================================
        const penaltyResult = applyPenalties(payoutAfterMultiplier, plan.penalties, employee, transactions, db);
        //   penaltyEngine logic:
        //     For each penalty rule, check trigger condition:
        //       e.g., return_pct > 10 triggers 'High Return Penalty'
        //     Apply penalty:
        //       PERCENTAGE_DEDUCTION: payout *= (1 - penalty_value/100)
        //       FIXED_DEDUCTION:      payout -= penalty_value
        //       KPI_DISQUALIFY:       zero out specific KPI
        //       PLAN_DISQUALIFY:      zero out entire payout

        let payoutAfterPenalty = penaltyResult.adjustedPayout;
        let penaltyAmount = payoutAfterMultiplier - payoutAfterPenalty;
        auditTrail.step9 = penaltyResult;

        // =============================================
        // STEP 10: Apply Cap
        // =============================================
        const capResult = applyCaps(payoutAfterPenalty, plan.caps, employee, transactions, db);
        //   capEngine logic:
        //     For each cap rule:
        //       MAX_PER_KPI:     cap individual KPI payouts (already enforced per-KPI if specified)
        //       MAX_PER_PLAN:    if payout > cap_value, payout = cap_value
        //       PCT_OF_SALARY:   maxPayout = employee.base_salary * (cap_value/100)
        //       PCT_OF_SALES:    maxPayout = employee_total_sales * (cap_value/100)
        //       BUDGET_CAP:      check running total against budget
        //       REGIONAL_CAP:    check territory running total
        //     Apply the MOST RESTRICTIVE cap

        let netPayout = capResult.adjustedPayout;
        let capAdjustment = payoutAfterPenalty - netPayout;
        auditTrail.step10 = capResult;

        // =============================================
        // CHECK ELIGIBILITY (applied as a gate -- can zero everything)
        // =============================================
        const eligibilityResult = checkEligibility(plan.eligibility, employee, transactions, db);
        //   eligibilityEngine logic:
        //     For each eligibility rule:
        //       Compute the metric for this employee from transactions
        //       Compare with threshold using operator
        //       If fails:
        //         ZERO_PAYOUT:           netPayout = 0
        //         PERCENTAGE_REDUCTION:  netPayout *= (1 - failure_value/100)
        //         KPI_DISQUALIFY:        zero the specific KPI (handled upstream)

        if (!eligibilityResult.isEligible) {
            netPayout = eligibilityResult.adjustedPayout !== undefined
                ? eligibilityResult.adjustedPayout
                : 0;
        }
        auditTrail.eligibility = eligibilityResult;

        // =============================================
        // STEP 11: Store Payout
        // =============================================
        const employeePayout = storeEmployeePayout(db, {
            calculationRunId: run.id,
            employeeId: employee.id,
            planId,
            periodStart,
            periodEnd,
            grossPayout,
            multiplierAmount,
            penaltyAmount,
            capAdjustment,
            netPayout,
            isEligible: eligibilityResult.isEligible,
            eligibilityFailures: eligibilityResult.failures,
        });

        // Store per-KPI results
        for (const kr of kpiResults) {
            storeKpiResult(db, {
                employeePayoutId: employeePayout.id,
                ...kr,
                calculationDetails: JSON.stringify(auditTrail),
            });
        }

        // =============================================
        // HANDLE SPLITS (if applicable)
        // =============================================
        if (plan.splits.length > 0) {
            applySplits(db, plan.splits, employeePayout, employee);
            //   splitEngine logic:
            //     For each split rule:
            //       Find the split participants
            //       Calculate each participant's share:
            //         PERCENTAGE: participant_payout = netPayout * (allocation_value / 100)
            //         FIXED:      participant_payout = allocation_value
            //       Create additional employee_payout records for split beneficiaries
            //       Reduce original employee's payout proportionally
        }

        allPayouts.push(employeePayout);

        // =============================================
        // STEP 12: Create Approval Entries
        // =============================================
        if (!isSimulation) {
            createApprovalEntry(db, employeePayout.id, 'SUBMITTED', 'SYSTEM');
        }

        // Write audit trail
        insertAuditTrail(db, {
            entityType: 'PAYOUT',
            entityId: employeePayout.id,
            action: isSimulation ? 'SIMULATE' : 'CALCULATE',
            changedBy: 'SYSTEM',
            newValues: JSON.stringify(auditTrail),
            description: `Calculation for ${employee.name}, period ${periodStart} to ${periodEnd}`,
        });
    }

    // =============================================
    // STEP 13: Lock Period (only on final approval, not during calculation)
    // =============================================
    // Lock is triggered separately via the approval workflow endpoint
    // When all payouts in a run are HR_APPROVED -> lock

    updateCalculationRunStatus(db, run.id, 'COMPLETED');

    return { runId: run.id, payoutCount: allPayouts.length, payouts: allPayouts };
}
```

### Slab Engine Detail (slabEngine.js)

```javascript
function determineSlabTier(slabSet, achievementPct) {
    const tiers = slabSet.tiers; // sorted by tier_order

    if (slabSet.slab_type === 'STEP') {
        // Find the single tier that contains this achievement
        for (const tier of tiers) {
            const inRange = achievementPct >= tier.range_from
                && (tier.range_to === null || achievementPct < tier.range_to);
            if (inRange) {
                return {
                    tierId: tier.id,
                    label: tier.label,
                    payoutType: tier.payout_type,
                    effectiveRate: tier.payout_value,
                };
            }
        }
        // Below minimum tier = 0
        return { tierId: null, label: 'Below Minimum', payoutType: 'PERCENTAGE', effectiveRate: 0 };
    }

    if (slabSet.slab_type === 'PROGRESSIVE') {
        // Each tier pays only on the portion within its range
        let totalPayout = 0;
        let lastTier = null;
        for (const tier of tiers) {
            if (achievementPct <= tier.range_from) break;
            const upperBound = tier.range_to !== null
                ? Math.min(achievementPct, tier.range_to)
                : achievementPct;
            const portionInTier = upperBound - tier.range_from;
            totalPayout += portionInTier * (tier.payout_value / 100);
            lastTier = tier;
        }
        return {
            tierId: lastTier?.id,
            label: lastTier?.label || 'N/A',
            payoutType: 'PERCENTAGE',
            effectiveRate: totalPayout,  // this is already the computed progressive amount
            isProgressive: true,
        };
    }

    if (slabSet.slab_type === 'ACCELERATOR') {
        // Normal rate up to 100%, then accelerated rate above 100%
        // Tier 1: 0-100% at base rate
        // Tier 2: 100%+ at accelerated rate
        let totalPayout = 0;
        for (const tier of tiers) {
            if (achievementPct <= tier.range_from) break;
            const upperBound = tier.range_to !== null
                ? Math.min(achievementPct, tier.range_to)
                : achievementPct;
            const portionInTier = upperBound - tier.range_from;
            totalPayout += portionInTier * (tier.payout_value / 100);
        }
        return {
            tierId: tiers[tiers.length - 1]?.id,
            label: achievementPct > 100 ? 'Accelerator' : 'Base',
            payoutType: 'PERCENTAGE',
            effectiveRate: totalPayout,
            isAccelerator: true,
        };
    }

    // DECELERATOR and REVERSE_PENALTY follow similar patterns
    // with different rate applications
}
```

```

### 5.3 State Management

Use **Zustand** (lightweight, no boilerplate) with these stores:

```javascript
// store/store.js
import { create } from 'zustand';

export const useAppStore = create((set) => ({
    // Current user persona (for demo switching)
    currentUser: null,        // { id, name, role, territory }
    setCurrentUser: (user) => set({ currentUser: user }),

    // Current period
    currentPeriod: { start: '2025-01-01', end: '2025-01-31' },
    setCurrentPeriod: (period) => set({ currentPeriod: period }),

    // Active plan (when in plan builder)
    activePlan: null,
    setActivePlan: (plan) => set({ activePlan: plan }),

    // Calculation run state
    calculationRunning: false,
    currentStep: 0,
    setCalculationState: (running, step) => set({ calculationRunning: running, currentStep: step }),
}));
```

### 5.4 UI Library

Use **Tailwind CSS** + **shadcn/ui** components (or Ant Design if preferred). For charts, use **Recharts** (React-native charting, good for dashboards).

---

## 6. Seed Data Description

### 6.1 Roles (10 records)

| Code | Name | Level |
|------|------|-------|
| PRE_SALES_REP | Pre-Sales Representative | FIELD |
| VAN_SALES_REP | Van Sales Representative | FIELD |
| DELIVERY_DRIVER | Delivery Driver | FIELD |
| MERCHANDISER | Merchandiser | FIELD |
| SALES_SUPERVISOR | Sales Supervisor | SUPERVISOR |
| ASM | Area Sales Manager | MANAGER |
| RSM | Regional Sales Manager | MANAGER |
| NSM | National Sales Manager | DIRECTOR |
| TRADE_MARKETING_EXEC | Trade Marketing Executive | FIELD |
| KEY_ACCOUNT_EXEC | Key Account Executive | FIELD |

### 6.2 Territories (8 records)

- National (parent: NULL)
  - North Region
    - North Territory A
    - North Territory B
  - South Region
    - South Territory A
    - South Territory B
  - East Region
  - West Region

### 6.3 Products (20 records across categories)

| Category | Example SKUs |
|----------|-------------|
| Beverages | Cola 500ml, Juice 1L, Water 2L, Energy Drink 250ml |
| Snacks | Chips Regular, Chips Large, Crackers, Nuts Pack |
| Dairy | Milk 1L, Yogurt 200ml, Cheese Slice, Butter 200g |
| Personal Care | Shampoo 200ml, Soap Bar, Deodorant |
| Home Care | Detergent 1kg, Dish Wash 500ml, Floor Cleaner |

5 products marked `is_strategic = 1`, 3 marked `is_new_launch = 1`.

### 6.4 Customers (15 records)

Mix of General Trade (8), Modern Trade (3), Key Account (2), Wholesale (2). Spread across territories. Channels: Retail (10), HORECA (3), Institution (2).

### 6.5 Employees (12 records)

| # | Role | Territory | Reports To |
|---|------|-----------|------------|
| 1 | Pre-Sales Rep | North A | Sales Supervisor |
| 2 | Pre-Sales Rep | North B | Sales Supervisor |
| 3 | Van Sales Rep | South A | Sales Supervisor |
| 4 | Van Sales Rep | South B | Sales Supervisor |
| 5 | Delivery Driver | North A | Sales Supervisor |
| 6 | Merchandiser | South A | Sales Supervisor |
| 7 | Sales Supervisor | North Region | ASM |
| 8 | Sales Supervisor | South Region | ASM |
| 9 | ASM | National | RSM |
| 10 | RSM | National | NSM |
| 11 | Trade Marketing Exec | North Region | ASM |
| 12 | Key Account Exec | National | RSM |

Base salaries ranging from 15,000 to 120,000 (local currency).

### 6.6 KPI Definitions (15 representative KPIs)

| # | Code | Category | Unit | Direction | Roles |
|---|------|----------|------|-----------|-------|
| 1 | TOTAL_SALES_VALUE | SALES | CURRENCY | HIGHER_IS_BETTER | All field + supervisor |
| 2 | TOTAL_SALES_VOLUME | SALES | COUNT | HIGHER_IS_BETTER | All field + supervisor |
| 3 | AVG_ORDER_VALUE | SALES | CURRENCY | HIGHER_IS_BETTER | Pre-Sales, Van Sales, Key Account |
| 4 | ECO (Effective Coverage Outlets) | DISTRIBUTION | COUNT | HIGHER_IS_BETTER | Pre-Sales, Van Sales |
| 5 | NEW_CUSTOMER_COUNT | DISTRIBUTION | COUNT | HIGHER_IS_BETTER | Pre-Sales, Key Account |
| 6 | LINES_PER_CALL | PRODUCTIVITY | RATIO | HIGHER_IS_BETTER | Pre-Sales, Van Sales |
| 7 | STRIKE_RATE | PRODUCTIVITY | PERCENTAGE | HIGHER_IS_BETTER | Pre-Sales, Van Sales |
| 8 | COLLECTION_PCT | FINANCIAL | PERCENTAGE | HIGHER_IS_BETTER | Pre-Sales, Van Sales |
| 9 | DSO (Days Sales Outstanding) | FINANCIAL | COUNT | LOWER_IS_BETTER | Pre-Sales, Key Account |
| 10 | RETURN_PCT | FINANCIAL | PERCENTAGE | LOWER_IS_BETTER | All field |
| 11 | PLANOGRAM_COMPLIANCE | MERCHANDISING | PERCENTAGE | HIGHER_IS_BETTER | Merchandiser |
| 12 | STRATEGIC_SKU_PCT | TRADE_MARKETING | PERCENTAGE | HIGHER_IS_BETTER | Trade Marketing, Pre-Sales |
| 13 | DELIVERY_ON_TIME_PCT | PRODUCTIVITY | PERCENTAGE | HIGHER_IS_BETTER | Delivery Driver |
| 14 | VISIT_COMPLIANCE | BEHAVIORAL | PERCENTAGE | HIGHER_IS_BETTER | All field |
| 15 | NEAR_EXPIRY_PCT | INVENTORY | PERCENTAGE | LOWER_IS_BETTER | Van Sales, Delivery Driver |

### 6.7 Commission Plans (2 demo plans)

**Plan A: "Field Sales - January 2025"**
- Roles: Pre-Sales Rep, Van Sales Rep
- Territory: All
- KPIs: TOTAL_SALES_VALUE (30%), ECO (20%), COLLECTION_PCT (15%), LINES_PER_CALL (10%), STRATEGIC_SKU_PCT (10%), RETURN_PCT (10%), VISIT_COMPLIANCE (5%)
- Slab type: Progressive for TOTAL_SALES_VALUE, Step for others
- Eligibility: Min sales >= 50,000; Min collection >= 70%
- Multiplier: Growth > 10% => 1.15x; Strategic SKU > 20% => 1.10x
- Penalty: Return % > 15% => 10% deduction
- Cap: Max 50% of base salary
- Split: Pre-Sales 70% / Delivery 30% on sales orders

**Plan B: "Supervisor Bonus - Q1 2025"**
- Roles: Sales Supervisor
- Territory: All
- KPIs: TOTAL_SALES_VALUE (40%), ECO (25%), COLLECTION_PCT (20%), NEW_CUSTOMER_COUNT (15%)
- Slab type: Accelerator for TOTAL_SALES_VALUE (extra rate above 100%)
- Eligibility: Team min sales >= 500,000
- Multiplier: Team growth > 15% => 1.20x
- Penalty: Any team member return > 20% => 5% deduction per member
- Cap: Max 75% of base salary

### 6.8 Transactions (~500 records)

Generate 500 transactions for January 2025 covering:
- Sales (SALE): ~300 records spread across employees, customers, products
- Returns (RETURN): ~40 records (to create return % data)
- Collections (COLLECTION): ~80 records (to create collection % data)
- Visits (VISIT): ~60 records (for strike rate, visit compliance)
- Deliveries (DELIVERY): ~20 records (for delivery driver KPIs)

Distribution designed so that:
- 2-3 employees hit >100% achievement (to test accelerators)
- 1-2 employees are below minimum threshold (to test eligibility failures)
- 1 employee has high return % (to trigger penalty)
- Most employees land in middle slabs (realistic distribution)

---

## 7. Prototype Scope: Include vs Defer

### IN SCOPE (Prototype V1)

| Feature | Depth |
|---------|-------|
| Commission Plan CRUD | Full: create, edit, clone, version, activate |
| Plan Builder (tabbed UI) | Full: all 8 tabs (KPIs, Slabs, Rules, Eligibility, Multipliers, Penalties, Caps, Splits) |
| KPI Library | 15 KPIs across all 9 categories, CRUD |
| Slab Engine | Step + Progressive + Accelerator (3 of 5 types) |
| Include/Exclude Rules | Product (category, SKU, brand) and Customer (type, channel) dimensions |
| Eligibility Engine | Min sales, min collection %, max return % (3 metrics) |
| Multiplier Engine | Growth + Strategic SKU (2 types), all 3 stacking modes |
| Penalty Engine | High return % + DSO triggers, percentage deduction type |
| Capping | Max per plan + % of salary (2 types) |
| Split Commission | Role-based percentage split (Pre-Sales/Delivery) |
| Calculation Pipeline | All 13 steps, fully working end-to-end |
| Pipeline Visualizer | Step-by-step progress UI with expandable details |
| Simulation | What-if with target adjustment sliders, budget impact summary |
| Salesperson Dashboard | Target vs achievement, slab progress, payout breakdown |
| Manager Dashboard | Team summary, KPI heatmap, ranking |
| Executive Dashboard | Incentive % of sales, growth vs payout trend |
| Approval Workflow | 3-stage (Manager -> Finance -> HR -> Lock) |
| Audit Trail | Full calculation breakdown, immutable log |
| Demo Persona Switching | Header dropdown to view as different roles |

### DEFERRED (Marked "Coming Soon" in UI)

| Feature | Reason |
|---------|--------|
| Decelerator slabs | Low priority slab type for prototype |
| Reverse/penalty slabs | Low priority slab type for prototype |
| Geography dimension in rules | 2 dimensions sufficient for demo |
| Org hierarchy dimension in rules | 2 dimensions sufficient for demo |
| Transaction hierarchy dimension | 2 dimensions sufficient for demo |
| Conditional IF-THEN-EXCEPT rules | Complex; basic include/exclude sufficient |
| Attribute-based tagging rules | Tag field exists but advanced matching deferred |
| Min active days eligibility | 3 eligibility metrics sufficient |
| Min visit count eligibility | 3 eligibility metrics sufficient |
| New Launch multiplier | 2 multiplier types sufficient |
| Territory multiplier | 2 multiplier types sufficient |
| Campaign multiplier | 2 multiplier types sufficient |
| Fixed deduction penalty | Percentage sufficient for demo |
| KPI/Plan disqualification penalty | Percentage sufficient for demo |
| Max per KPI cap | Plan-level cap sufficient |
| Budget cap tracking | Complex running-total logic deferred |
| Regional cap | Plan-level cap sufficient |
| Shared credit split | Role split sufficient |
| Pool distribution split | Role split sufficient |
| Frequency: Quarterly | Monthly sufficient for demo |
| Real authentication/authorization | Use demo persona switcher instead |
| File export (PDF/Excel) | Display only for prototype |
| Email notifications | Not needed for demo |
| Bulk plan operations | One-at-a-time sufficient |
| API rate limiting / caching | Not needed for prototype |

---

## 8. Implementation Sequence

The recommended build order, structured to always have something demoable.

**Phase 1: Foundation (Day 1-2)**
1. Initialize monorepo (server + client) with package.json scripts
2. Set up SQLite with better-sqlite3 (synchronous, fast, simple)
3. Create all tables via schema.js
4. Implement seed.js with all reference data + transactions
5. Set up Express with basic error handling, CORS
6. Set up Vite + React + Tailwind + Recharts
7. Build AppShell, Sidebar, Header, routing skeleton

**Phase 2: Reference Data APIs + KPI Library (Day 2-3)**
1. Implement CRUD for roles, employees, territories, products, customers
2. Implement KPI definitions CRUD
3. Build KpiLibraryPage with category filtering
4. Build basic DataTable shared component

**Phase 3: Plan Builder (Day 3-5)**
1. Implement commission_plans CRUD API
2. Build PlanListPage and PlanCard
3. Build PlanBuilderPage shell with tabs
4. Implement plan_kpis API + KpiSelector + WeightAllocator
5. Implement slab_sets/tiers API + SlabConfigurator with validation
6. Implement rules API + IncludeExcludeEditor
7. Implement eligibility API + EligibilityRuleEditor
8. Implement multipliers API + MultiplierRuleEditor
9. Implement penalties API + PenaltyRuleEditor
10. Implement caps API + CapRuleEditor
11. Implement splits API + SplitRuleEditor

**Phase 4: Calculation Engine (Day 5-7)**
1. Build calculationPipeline.js orchestrator
2. Implement each of the 13 steps as individual modules
3. Build slabEngine.js with Step + Progressive + Accelerator
4. Build ruleEngine.js for include/exclude filtering
5. Build eligibilityEngine.js
6. Build multiplierEngine.js
7. Build penaltyEngine.js
8. Build capEngine.js
9. Build splitEngine.js
10. Implement calculation API endpoint
11. Build CalculationRunPage with PipelineVisualizer
12. Build CalculationBreakdown detail view

**Phase 5: Simulation (Day 7-8)**
1. Extend calculation engine to accept overrides (is_simulation flag)
2. Build simulation API endpoints
3. Build SimulationPage with WhatIfSliders
4. Build SimulationResults with comparison charts

**Phase 6: Dashboards (Day 8-9)**
1. Build dashboard API endpoints with aggregation queries
2. Build DashboardSalesperson (charts + cards)
3. Build DashboardManager (heatmap + team table)
4. Build DashboardExecutive (trend charts)
5. Implement persona switcher in Header

**Phase 7: Approval + Audit (Day 9-10)**
1. Build approval workflow API (approve, reject, lock)
2. Build ApprovalPage with queue and detail views
3. Build RejectDialog with mandatory reason
4. Build AuditTrailPage with filtering
5. Build CalculationBreakdown (linked from audit)

**Phase 8: Polish (Day 10-11)**
1. Add "Coming Soon" badges on deferred features
2. Add loading states, empty states, error states
3. Responsive layout fixes
4. Seed data verification (run calculation, verify results make sense)
5. README with setup instructions and demo walkthrough

---

## 9. Key Architectural Decisions

**1. SQLite with better-sqlite3 (synchronous):** For a prototype, synchronous SQLite is simpler to reason about, especially in the 13-step calculation pipeline where each step depends on the previous. No need for connection pools or async/await complexity. The entire calculation for 12 employees runs in < 100ms.

**2. JSON columns for flexibility:** Fields like `match_values`, `applicable_roles`, `tags`, `calculation_details`, `metadata` use TEXT storing JSON. This avoids junction table explosion while keeping the schema manageable. SQLite's `json_extract()` function can be used for queries.

**3. Calculation pipeline as explicit step modules:** Each of the 13 steps is a separate file. This makes the non-negotiable order visible in the code structure, makes each step independently testable, and makes the audit trail natural (each step writes its output to the audit object).

**4. Simulation reuses the real engine:** The simulation endpoint calls the exact same `runCalculation()` function with `isSimulation = true`. This guarantees simulation accuracy. The only difference is that simulation results are stored with `is_simulation = 1` and never enter the approval workflow.

**5. Eligibility checked late but can zero everything:** Eligibility is evaluated after the main pipeline because it needs the same transaction data. If eligibility fails, the net_payout is zeroed or reduced. This matches the document's requirement that eligibility failure can mean zero payout.

**6. Persona switcher instead of auth:** The demo uses a dropdown in the header to switch between employees/roles. This lets a demo audience see all dashboard views (salesperson, manager, executive) without implementing real authentication.

**7. Audit trail is append-only:** The audit_trail table has no UPDATE or DELETE operations exposed. Every change creates a new row. The approval_log is similarly immutable. This matches the document's requirement for no deletion.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/Users/fci/Desktop/Prakash/Commission/server/src/engine/calculationPipeline.js` - The orchestrator that implements the non-negotiable 13-step sequence. This is the centerpiece of the entire system and the most complex piece of logic.
- `/Users/fci/Desktop/Prakash/Commission/server/src/engine/slabEngine.js` - The slab determination engine supporting Step, Progressive, and Accelerator types. Critical for correct payout calculation at step 4-5.
- `/Users/fci/Desktop/Prakash/Commission/server/src/db/schema.js` - All table creation DDL. Must be built first as everything depends on the data model. The ~20 tables defined above form the entire data foundation.
- `/Users/fci/Desktop/Prakash/Commission/client/src/pages/PlanBuilderPage.jsx` - The most complex UI page with 8 tabs for configuring every aspect of a commission plan. This is where users will spend most of their time.
- `/Users/fci/Desktop/Prakash/Commission/client/src/components/calculation/PipelineVisualizer.jsx` - The visual representation of the 13-step pipeline running in real-time. This is the key "wow factor" component for the demo that proves the engine works end-to-end."

```

### 2.3 Commission Plans

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- COMMISSION PLANS (the header)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE commission_plans (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    name            TEXT NOT NULL,
    version         INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    status          TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DRAFT',  -- 'DRAFT','ACTIVE','EXPIRED','ARCHIVED'
    valid_from      TEXT NOT NULL,                   -- date
    valid_to        TEXT NOT NULL,                   -- date
    frequency       TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'MONTHLY', -- 'MONTHLY','QUARTERLY'
    description     TEXT,
    created_by      TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    parent_plan_id  INTEGER REFERENCES commission_plans(id)  -- for versioning lineage
);

-- ============================================================
-- PLAN ROLE MAPPING (which roles this plan applies to)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE plan_roles (
    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    role_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES roles(id),
    UNIQUE(plan_id, role_id)
);

-- ============================================================
-- PLAN TERRITORY MAPPING
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE plan_territories (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    territory_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES territories(id),
    UNIQUE(plan_id, territory_id)
);

-- ============================================================
-- PLAN KPI CONFIG (KPIs attached to a plan, with targets & weights)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE plan_kpis (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    kpi_id          INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES kpi_definitions(id),
    target_value    REAL NOT NULL,               -- the 100% target
    weight          REAL NOT NULL,               -- e.g., 0.25 for 25%
    min_threshold   REAL DEFAULT 0,              -- below this = 0 achievement
    max_cap_pct     REAL DEFAULT 200,            -- achievement cap (200 = 200%)
    base_payout     REAL NOT NULL,               -- payout at 100% achievement
    sort_order      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    UNIQUE(plan_id, kpi_id)
);
```

```sql

    label           TEXT,                      -- human label: "Bronze", "Silver", "Gold"
    UNIQUE(slab_set_id, tier_order)
);
```

### 2.6 Eligibility Rules

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- ELIGIBILITY RULES (gate checks before payout)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE eligibility_rules (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    name            TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'Minimum Sales Threshold'
    metric          TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'MIN_SALES_VALUE', 'MIN_COLLECTION_PCT',
                                               -- 'MAX_RETURN_PCT', 'MIN_ACTIVE_DAYS',
                                               -- 'MIN_VISIT_COUNT'
    operator        TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'GTE', 'LTE', 'GT', 'LT', 'EQ'
    threshold_value REAL NOT NULL,             -- e.g., 50000 for min sales, 80 for min collection%
    failure_action  TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'ZERO_PAYOUT', 'PERCENTAGE_REDUCTION', 'KPI_DISQUALIFY'
    failure_value   REAL DEFAULT 0,            -- reduction %: e.g., 50 means reduce by 50%
    applies_to_kpi_id INTEGER REFERENCES plan_kpis(id),  -- NULL = entire plan
    sort_order      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
```

### 2.7 Multiplier Rules

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- MULTIPLIER RULES
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE multiplier_rules (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    name            TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'Growth Multiplier', 'Strategic SKU Bonus'
    type            TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'GROWTH', 'STRATEGIC_SKU', 'NEW_LAUNCH',
                                               -- 'TERRITORY', 'CAMPAIGN', 'ACCELERATOR'
    condition_field TEXT NOT NULL,             -- what triggers it: 'growth_pct', 'strategic_sku_pct', etc.
    condition_operator TEXT NOT NULL,          -- 'GTE', 'GT', 'BETWEEN'
    condition_value TEXT NOT NULL,             -- single value or JSON [min, max]
    multiplier_value REAL NOT NULL,            -- e.g., 1.25 = 25% boost
    stacking_mode   TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'MULTIPLICATIVE',  -- 'ADDITIVE', 'MULTIPLICATIVE', 'HIGHEST_ONLY'
    applies_to_kpi_id INTEGER REFERENCES plan_kpis(id),  -- NULL = entire plan
    sort_order      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
```

### 2.8 Penalty Rules

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- PENALTY RULES
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE penalty_rules (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    name            TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'High Return Penalty'
    trigger_metric  TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'RETURN_PCT', 'DSO', 'AUDIT_SCORE'
    trigger_operator TEXT NOT NULL,            -- 'GT', 'GTE', 'LT', 'LTE'
    trigger_value   REAL NOT NULL,             -- e.g., 10 for return_pct > 10%
    penalty_type    TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'PERCENTAGE_DEDUCTION', 'FIXED_DEDUCTION',
                                               -- 'KPI_DISQUALIFY', 'PLAN_DISQUALIFY'
    penalty_value   REAL NOT NULL,             -- deduction % or fixed amount
    applies_to_kpi_id INTEGER REFERENCES plan_kpis(id),  -- NULL = entire plan
    sort_order      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
```

### 2.9 Capping Rules

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- CAPPING RULES
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE capping_rules (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    name            TEXT NOT NULL,
    cap_type        TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'MAX_PER_KPI', 'MAX_PER_PLAN',
                                               -- 'PCT_OF_SALARY', 'PCT_OF_SALES',
                                               -- 'BUDGET_CAP', 'REGIONAL_CAP'
    cap_value       REAL NOT NULL,             -- absolute amount or percentage
    reference_field TEXT,                      -- 'base_salary', 'total_sales' (for PCT types)
    applies_to_kpi_id INTEGER REFERENCES plan_kpis(id),  -- NULL = plan-level cap
    territory_id    INTEGER REFERENCES territories(id),   -- for regional caps
    sort_order      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
```

### 2.10 Split Commission

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- SPLIT COMMISSION RULES
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE split_rules (

    plan_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),

    split_type      TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'ROLE_SPLIT', 'SHARED_CREDIT', 'SUPERVISOR_OVERRIDE',

-- SPLIT PARTICIPANTS
-- ============================================================

    split_rule_id   INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES split_rules(id),
    role_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES roles(id),

    UNIQUE(split_rule_id, role_id)
);

### 2.11 Transactions (Source Data)

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- SALES TRANSACTIONS (the raw data pipeline feeds)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE transactions (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    transaction_code TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    transaction_date TEXT NOT NULL,            -- date
    transaction_type TEXT NOT NULL,            -- 'SALE', 'RETURN', 'COLLECTION', 'VISIT',
                                               -- 'DELIVERY', 'CREDIT_NOTE'
    employee_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES employees(id),
    customer_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES customers(id),
    product_id      INTEGER REFERENCES products(id),       -- NULL for visits/collections
    territory_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES territories(id),
    quantity        REAL DEFAULT 0,
    unit_price      REAL DEFAULT 0,
    total_amount    REAL DEFAULT 0,
    payment_status  TEXT,                     -- 'PAID', 'PENDING', 'OVERDUE'
    days_outstanding INTEGER DEFAULT 0,       -- for DSO calculation
    is_new_customer INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    is_new_product  INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    metadata        TEXT,                     -- JSON for extensible fields
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

CREATE INDEX idx_transactions_employee ON transactions(employee_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_transactions_date ON transactions(transaction_date);
CREATE INDEX idx_transactions_type ON transactions(transaction_type);
```

### 2.12 Calculation Results

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- CALCULATION RUNS (a batch calculation execution)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE calculation_runs (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    plan_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    period_start    TEXT NOT NULL,
    period_end      TEXT NOT NULL,
    status          TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',  -- 'PENDING','RUNNING','COMPLETED','FAILED'
    is_simulation   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,       -- 1 = what-if, don't persist as real
    triggered_by    TEXT NOT NULL,
    started_at      TEXT,
    completed_at    TEXT,
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

-- ============================================================
-- EMPLOYEE PAYOUTS (final per-employee per-plan result)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE employee_payouts (
    id                  INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    calculation_run_id  INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES calculation_runs(id),
    employee_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES employees(id),
    plan_id             INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
    period_start        TEXT NOT NULL,
    period_end          TEXT NOT NULL,
    -- Calculation results
    gross_payout        REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   -- after all KPIs aggregated
    multiplier_amount   REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   -- total multiplier impact
    penalty_amount      REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   -- total penalty deduction
    cap_adjustment      REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   -- amount removed by capping
    net_payout          REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   -- final payable amount
    -- Eligibility
    is_eligible         INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    eligibility_failures TEXT,                      -- JSON array of failed rule names
    -- Approval
    approval_status     TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',  -- 'PENDING','MANAGER_APPROVED',
                                                          -- 'FINANCE_APPROVED','HR_APPROVED',
                                                          -- 'LOCKED','REJECTED'
    is_locked           INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    locked_at           TEXT,
    created_at          TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

-- ============================================================
-- KPI RESULTS (per-employee per-KPI breakdown within a run)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE kpi_results (
    id                  INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    employee_payout_id  INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES employee_payouts(id),
    plan_kpi_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES plan_kpis(id),
    -- Step 1-2: Scoped data
    scoped_transaction_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    -- Step 3: KPI achievement
    actual_value        REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    target_value        REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    achievement_pct     REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,      -- (actual/target)*100
    -- Step 4: Slab
    slab_tier_id        INTEGER REFERENCES slab_tiers(id),
    slab_label          TEXT,
    slab_rate           REAL DEFAULT 0,
    -- Step 5: KPI payout
    raw_kpi_payout      REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    -- Step 6: Weighted
    weight              REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    weighted_payout     REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    -- Audit details
    calculation_details TEXT,                          -- JSON: full step-by-step breakdown
    created_at          TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
```

### 2.13 Approval Workflow & Audit Trail

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- APPROVAL LOG (immutable history)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE approval_log (
    id                  INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    employee_payout_id  INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES employee_payouts(id),
    action              TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'SUBMITTED', 'MANAGER_APPROVED', 'MANAGER_REJECTED',
                                                   -- 'FINANCE_APPROVED', 'FINANCE_REJECTED',
                                                   -- 'HR_APPROVED', 'HR_REJECTED', 'LOCKED'
    performed_by        TEXT NOT NULL,             -- user/role who performed the action
    comment             TEXT,                      -- mandatory for rejections
    previous_status     TEXT NOT NULL,
    new_status          TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at          TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

-- ============================================================
-- AUDIT TRAIL (system-wide change log)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE audit_trail (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    entity_type     TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'PLAN', 'KPI', 'SLAB', 'RULE', 'PAYOUT', etc.
    entity_id       INTEGER NOT NULL,
    action          TEXT NOT NULL,             -- 'CREATE', 'UPDATE', 'ACTIVATE', 'CALCULATE', etc.
    changed_by      TEXT NOT NULL,
    old_values      TEXT,                      -- JSON snapshot of previous state
    new_values      TEXT,                      -- JSON snapshot of new state
    description     TEXT,                      -- Human-readable change description
    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

CREATE INDEX idx_audit_entity ON audit_trail(entity_type, entity_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_time ON audit_trail(created_at);
```

### 2.14 Simulation Snapshots

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- SIMULATION SNAPSHOTS (what-if scenarios -- never posted to real payouts)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE simulation_snapshots (
    id                  INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    calculation_run_id  INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES calculation_runs(id),
    scenario_name       TEXT NOT NULL,
    parameters          TEXT NOT NULL,          -- JSON: what-if overrides
    summary             TEXT NOT NULL,          -- JSON: budget impact, avg payout, etc.
    created_by          TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at          TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
```

## 3. Backend API Endpoints

### 3.1 Plans

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET    | `/api/plans` | List all plans (with status filter) |
| GET    | `/api/plans/:id` | Get plan with full config (KPIs, slabs, rules, etc.) |
| POST   | `/api/plans` | Create new plan |
| PUT    | `/api/plans/:id` | Update plan |
| POST   | `/api/plans/:id/clone` | Clone plan (create new version) |
| PATCH  | `/api/plans/:id/status` | Change plan status (DRAFT -> ACTIVE, etc.) |

### 3.2 KPI Library

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET    | `/api/kpis` | List all KPI definitions (filter by category, role) |
| GET    | `/api/kpis/:id` | Get single KPI definition |
| POST   | `/api/kpis` | Create new KPI definition |

### 3.3 Plan KPI Configuration

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET    | `/api/plans/:planId/kpis` | List KPIs configured for this plan |
| POST   | `/api/plans/:planId/kpis` | Add KPI to plan with target/weight/base_payout |
| PUT    | `/api/plans/:planId/kpis/:planKpiId` | Update KPI config |
| DELETE | `/api/plans/:planId/kpis/:planKpiId` | Remove KPI from plan |

### 3.4 Slab Configuration

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET    | `/api/plan-kpis/:planKpiId/slabs` | Get slab set + tiers for a plan KPI |
| POST   | `/api/plan-kpis/:planKpiId/slabs` | Create slab set with tiers |
| PUT    | `/api/slab-sets/:slabSetId` | Update slab set (replace tiers) |

### 3.5 Include/Exclude Rules

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET    | `/api/plans/:planId/rules` | List all rule sets for plan |
| POST   | `/api/plans/:planId/rules` | Create rule set with rules |
| PUT    | `/api/rule-sets/:ruleSetId` | Update rule set |
| DELETE | `/api/rule-sets/:ruleSetId` | Delete rule set |

### 3.6 Eligibility, Multiplier, Penalty, Cap, Split (identical pattern)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET    | `/api/plans/:planId/eligibility` | List eligibility rules |
| POST   | `/api/plans/:planId/eligibility` | Create eligibility rule |
| PUT    | `/api/eligibility/:id` | Update |
| DELETE | `/api/eligibility/:id` | Delete |
| GET    | `/api/plans/:planId/multipliers` | List multiplier rules |
| POST   | `/api/plans/:planId/multipliers` | Create |
| PUT    | `/api/multipliers/:id` | Update |
| DELETE | `/api/multipliers/:id` | Delete |
| GET    | `/api/plans/:planId/penalties` | List penalty rules |
| POST   | `/api/plans/:planId/penalties` | Create |
| PUT    | `/api/penalties/:id` | Update |
| DELETE | `/api/penalties/:id` | Delete |
| GET    | `/api/plans/:planId/caps` | List capping rules |
| POST   | `/api/plans/:planId/caps` | Create |
| PUT    | `/api/caps/:id` | Update |
| DELETE | `/api/caps/:id` | Delete |
| GET    | `/api/plans/:planId/splits` | List split rules |
| POST   | `/api/plans/:planId/splits` | Create with participants |
| PUT    | `/api/splits/:id` | Update |
| DELETE | `/api/splits/:id` | Delete |

### 3.7 Transactions (Source Data)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET    | `/api/transactions` | List (filter by date, employee, type) |
| POST   | `/api/transactions/bulk` | Bulk import (used by seed) |
| GET    | `/api/transactions/summary/:employeeId` | Aggregated summary for an employee |

### 3.8 Calculation Engine

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST   | `/api/calculate` | Trigger calculation: `{ planId, periodStart, periodEnd }` |
| GET    | `/api/calculation-runs` | List all runs |
| GET    | `/api/calculation-runs/:id` | Get run results with all employee payouts |
| GET    | `/api/calculation-runs/:id/employee/:empId` | Detailed breakdown for one employee |

### 3.9 Simulation

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST   | `/api/simulate` | Run simulation: `{ planId, periodStart, periodEnd, overrides }` |
| GET    | `/api/simulations` | List saved simulations |
| GET    | `/api/simulations/:id` | Get simulation detail |

The `overrides` object can modify: target values, slab rates, multiplier values, add/remove KPIs, adjust weights -- then the engine runs identically but stores results as `is_simulation = 1`.

### 3.10 Approval Workflow

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET    | `/api/approvals/pending` | Queue: payouts awaiting approval (filter by stage) |
| POST   | `/api/approvals/:payoutId/approve` | Approve (advance to next stage) |
| POST   | `/api/approvals/:payoutId/reject` | Reject with mandatory reason |
| POST   | `/api/approvals/:payoutId/lock` | Final lock after HR approval |
| GET    | `/api/approvals/:payoutId/history` | Approval log for a payout |

### 3.11 Dashboard

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET    | `/api/dashboard/salesperson/:empId` | Target vs achievement, slab, multipliers, penalties, estimated payout |
| GET    | `/api/dashboard/manager/:empId` | Team summary, KPI heatmap, budget utilization, ranking |
| GET    | `/api/dashboard/executive` | Incentive % of sales, growth vs payout trend, ROI |

### 3.12 Audit Trail

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET    | `/api/audit` | Query audit trail (filter by entity_type, entity_id, date range) |
| GET    | `/api/audit/calculation/:runId` | Full calculation audit for a run |

### 3.13 Reference Data

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET    | `/api/roles` | List all roles |
| GET    | `/api/employees` | List employees (filter by role, territory) |
| GET    | `/api/territories` | List territories
